name: Mabel Kirk's Butternut Squash Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Butternut squash, cut into 1-inch cubes, cooked and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. For the crust, combine flour and salt in a medium bowl. Cut shortening into the flour mixture using pastry blender or two knives until it forms pea-sized chunks. Sprinkle one tablespoon of water over one small section of the flour mixture and mix lightly with a fork. Repeat, rotating the bowl, one section at a time.
3. Form two-thirds of the dough into large balls, then press down to form a 5-6 inch diameter "pancake" (remainder can be rolled out, sprinkled with cinnamon and sugar, cut into pinwheels, brushed with butter and sugar-water glaze, and baked separately). Sprinkle flour on rolling surface (preferably taped-down pastry cloth) and, using a flour-dusted rolling pin, roll dough into a circle until it is about 1/4 inch thick. Trim 1 inch larger than an inverted 10-inch Pyrex pie plate. Carefully transfer the rolled pastry onto the right-side-up pie plate. Press into the pie plate. Fold the edge under. Flute the 1/2-inch high edge. Cover with plastic wrap. Refrigerate.
4. In a mixing bowl, combine all filling ingredients and mix at medium speed for 5 minutes. Pour the mixture into the chilled pastry shell.
5. Cover the edge of the pie with a long strip of aluminum foil to prevent over-browning. Bake for 10 minutes at 400°F. Reduce heat to 375°F and bake for another 25 minutes. Remove foil. Bake an additional 10-15 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean and the crust is golden brown.
